channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Cómo protegerte de las garrapatas
item.description: A medida que sube la temperatura global, las garrapatas se están expandiendo a nuevos lugares. Cada vez hay más de estos arácnidos, y pueden portar más enfermedades.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Astor

item.title: La fragilidad de la memoria, o cuando el canario de mi padre voló
item.description: En las etapas finales de su demencia, un recuerdo perdido de la infancia regresó, perfectamente formado. ¿Qué pasaba en su cerebro?
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry and Graham Dickie

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times

item.title: Cada vez más personas siguen consejos de salud de los influentes
item.description: Las redes sociales están plagadas de creadores de contenido de salud. El hecho de que muchos de ellos no tengan formación médica puede ser gran parte de su atractivo.
item.dc:creator: Todd Rosenberg

item.title: Bartenders at a Cocktail Mecca Propose a New Concoction: a Micro-Union
item.description: The bartenders at Attaboy, a Lower East Side joint considered one of the best cocktail bars in the world, voted to form a union.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:description: Attaboy empoloyees after the vote to unionize, from left: Jon Nutter, Hannah Chouinard, Zack Gelnaw-Rubin and Samaiyah Patrick.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 9, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How a Not-So-Nice Doctor on ‘The Pitt’ Taps Into Her Softer Side
item.description: Isa Briones, who plays Dr. Trinity Santos, is also starring in “Just in Time” on Broadway. She’s still getting used to all the love from her fans.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Alexandra Genova for The New York Times

item.title: China’s Exports and Imports Set Records in April Amid High Energy Costs
item.description: The country’s exports surged and its trade surplus with the United States widened ahead of President Trump’s scheduled visit next week to Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At the Beijing auto show in April, which featured hundreds of electric cars. Chinese electric vehicles are being exported in record numbers.

item.title: Milei y la motosierra que seduce a la derecha global
item.description: Con su cruzada desreguladora, Javier Milei se convirtió en una figura estelar del universo MAGA. Pero en Argentina, su experimento de ajuste extremo enfrenta un momento decisivo.
item.dc:creator: Roger Cohen

item.title: Peter Magyar Prepares to Take Over as Hungary’s Leader From Viktor Orban
item.description: The former opposition leader, who won a landslide election, now faces the formidable job of dismantling what his predecessor called an “illiberal democracy.”
item.dc:creator: Lili Rutai

media:content.media:description: Peter Magyar, the leader of the Tisza Party, in Budapest last month.

item.title: Punch, the Lonely Monkey Who Drew Crowds to a Small Japan Zoo, Is Growing Up. He’s Still a Star.
item.description: The macaque, now 9 months old, has been attracting tens of thousands of visitors to a small Japanese zoo. He ignores the attention.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Hisako Ueno and Kentaro Takahashi
item.category: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals

media:content.media:credit: Kentaro Takahashi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Punch has handled the limelight better than most young stars. Since first hitting the headlines he has slowly integrated into his troop and made friends — monkey and human alike.

item.title: Why the Trump-Xi Summit Matters, Even if Little Seems to Come of It
item.description: The war in Iran, trade, artificial intelligence and Taiwan are expected to be on the agenda. But expectations are modest.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: A Less Muscular Victory Day Parade Shows Putin’s Growing Vulnerability
item.description: The event, shrunken because of security fears, adds to a sense that Moscow and other cities can no longer be insulated from the war.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko

media:content.media:description: Police officers in central Moscow before the parade on Saturday.

item.title: How China’s Leader Lost Faith in His Generals
item.description: Xi Jinping spent 13 years building a military to rival that of the United States. But the stronger the Chinese forces grew, the less he trusted the generals he had handpicked to run them.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, during a military parade in Tiananmen Square in 2025 in Beijing.

item.title: In Hungary, Viktor Orban Loyalists Jump Ship
item.description: Peter Magyar, the incoming prime minister, has promised to push out “puppets of the old regime.” Many are jumping first.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai

media:content.media:description: Peter Magyar, the former opposition leader, last month in Budapest.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Adding Debt and Losing Sleep to See World Cup
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, May 9, 2026.

item.title: Dynamic Artistic Duos are on Display in New York This Spring
item.description: Three exhibitions are pairing artists to reveal how their works complement — or contrast with — each other.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

item.title: The Korean Artist Park Daesung Is In the Spotlight at 81
item.description: The Korean artist is nearly 81, but his works have been featured in shows in Chicago, London, Washington and Los Angeles. Now they are also headed to TEFAF New York.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher

media:content.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Park Daesung at his studio in Gyeongju, South Korea. Gana Art, a major gallery in Seoul, will take four of his paintings to TEFAF New York.

item.title: Man Dies in Subway Attack; Mamdani Orders Inquiry Into Suspect’s Release From Bellevue
item.description: A 76-year-old man died after he was pushed down subway stairs, and the police arrested a suspect who had been discharged from the public hospital’s psychiatric unit hours earlier.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Anusha Bayya

media:content.media:credit: an Rong Xu for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York called for an immediate investigation into the psychiatric evaluation and discharge of a man who the police said fatally assaulted another man five hours after his release.

item.title: Extra Hot
item.description: Katie Hoody’s latest Saturday themeless puzzle shows little mercy.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: You may want to lie down for a bit after solving this puzzle.

item.title: Corrections: May 9, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, May 9, 2026.

item.title: U.S. Conducts Third Boat Strike in 5 Days, Killing 2 and Leaving a Survivor
item.description: Of the 57 attacks in the Caribbean and eastern Pacific against boats accused of drug trafficking, there have rarely been survivors.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

media:content.media:description: A recent image from a video provided by the U.S. military shows what it said was a strike on a boat in the Caribbean.

item.title: Republicans Are Building an Advantage in Redistricting. How Much?
item.description: Where things stands in the race for House control after recent court rulings.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn

media:content.media:credit: Brad J. Vest/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: State Senator Charlane Oliver had a banner pulled from her hands as the Tennessee General Assembly gave approval to a congressional map intended to eliminate the state’s lone Democratic-held district.

item.title: David Attenborough, la voz de la naturaleza, cumple 100 años
item.description: Imágenes y momentos importantes de la trayectoria del que quizá sea el naturalista más célebre del mundo.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe

media:content.media:credit: John Stillwell/Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Mr. Attenborough enrolled at the University of Cambridge in 1945, where he studied natural sciences, including geology and zoology.

item.title: Iranian Propaganda vs. U.S. Talking Points: How We Determined the Real Damage to U.S. Military Bases
item.description: Amid the barrage of messages and misinformation swirling online and on Capitol Hill, about what damage U.S. military sites incurred during the conflict with Iran, a Times analysis of satellite imagery shows 18 sites in seven countries were hit.
item.dc:creator: Aric Toler, Devon Lum, Christoph Koettl, Natalie Reneau and Aaron Byrd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Jury Finds City of L.A. Not Liable in 2021 Police Shooting of Teen Girl
item.description: The death of Valentina Orellana-Peralta, a 14-year-old bystander, occurred at a store where the police were responding to reports of an assault.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

media:content.media:credit: Morgan Lieberman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Flowers and signs were placed on the sidewalk at a vigil for Valentina Orellana-Peralta and Daniel Elena Lopez in January 2022 at the Burlington store in North Hollywood, Calif.

item.title: Blake Lively Settlement Had No Financial Payout, Filing Confirms
item.description: Justin Baldoni’s company did not agree to make a payout as part of the deal that ended Ms. Lively’s lawsuit, but she is able to seek compensation through a separate legal avenue.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:description: As the legal dispute between Justin Baldoni and Blake Lively continues, a new court filing confirms their settlement agreement includes no financial payout to her. But it enables her to seek compensation through an alternative legal avenue.

item.title: Live: Ezra Klein Moderates California Housing Forum With Candidates for Governor
item.description: The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein moderates a forum with five of the top candidates for governor of California.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers and 13 crew members on the Caribbean Princess reported being ill, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: The Caribbean Princess left Port Everglades in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., on April 28 and is expected to return to Florida on Monday.

item.title: After Frederiksen Falls Short in Denmark, Right-Leaning Politician Is Asked to Form Government
item.description: King Frederik X has appointed a right-leaning politician to try to form the next government.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Emil Nicolai Helms/Ritzau Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Given high marks for handling President Trump, Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen of Denmark was seen as falling short when it came to domestic issues.

item.title: U.S. Targets Iran’s Missile and Drone Program With Sanctions
item.description: The new measures are focused on Chinese companies that supply Iran’s military with materials to make drones.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Friday.

item.title: Alabama Asks Supreme Court to Allow it to Use New Voting Map
item.description: State officials urged the justices to allow them to jettison Alabama’s congressional district map, citing the Supreme Court’s recent decision that dealt a blow to the Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:description: State representatives voting on a redistricting bill during a special session of the Alabama Legislature on Wednesday.

item.title: For a Third Time, Elderly Man Is Convicted of a Grisly Murder
item.description: A Brooklyn jury found Harvey Marcelin guilty of the 2022 murder of a woman. He’d been convicted twice before for killing women, starting in the 1960s.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Stephanie Keith for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Investigators found the victim’s remains spread throughout Brooklyn.

item.title: How Trump Is Prioritizing White People as Refugees
item.description: President Trump has upended the U.S. refugee program to prioritize mainly white Afrikaners. Our White House correspondent Zolan Kanno-Youngs reports he is now is now considering doubling the amount he allows into the country.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Gilad Thaler, Stephanie Swart, Jon Miller and Whitney Shefte

item.title: David Attenborough Celebrates His 100th Birthday
item.description: The filmmaker David Attenborough, who turned 100-years-old on Friday, has spent decades documenting and advocating for the natural world.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Fetherston and Christina Kelso

media:content.media:credit: John Stillwell/Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Mr. Attenborough enrolled at the University of Cambridge in 1945, where he studied natural sciences, including geology and zoology.

item.title: Starmer Takes Responsibility for Big Losses in U.K. Election Results
item.description: Britain’s Labour Party had lost close to 1,100 municipal council seats across England by Friday evening, while the right-wing populist Reform U.K. had gained close to 1,200.
item.dc:creator: Monika Cvorak and Jamie Leventhal

item.title: Lawmakers May Continue to Inspect ICE Detention Centers, Appeals Court Rules
item.description: A federal appeals court declined, for now, to halt an order requiring D.H.S. to permit unannounced visits to immigration detention centers by Democrats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Delaney Hall, a 1,000-person detention center used by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, in Newark, N.J.

item.title: Judge Rejects Defense Plea to Ban Cameras in Charlie Kirk Trial
item.description: The judge ruled on Friday that defense lawyers for Tyler Robinson had failed to prove that Utah law would support a blanket ban on news cameras.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:description: Judge Tony Graf of the Fourth District Court in Utah is presiding over the trial of the man accused of killing the conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: Facing Pressure, Trump Officials Reject Claims They’re Softening on Immigration
item.description: Immigration hard-liners have grown frustrated with the level of deportations and the Department of Homeland Security’s attempts, under its new secretary, to stay under the radar.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:description: Federal agents operating in Minneapolis in January. A growing share of Republicans now say the administration is doing too little on deportations, according to a poll released by Pew on Monday.

item.title: An Artist Who Sees the Beauty in Dust
item.description: Reggie Burrows Hodges discusses Malta, Caravaggio and a painting inspired by the winds of the Sahara.
item.dc:creator: Gazelle Mba

item.title: Edith Eva Eger, Psychologist Who Barely Survived Auschwitz, Dies at 98
item.description: Her time in concentration camps brought her an understanding of humanity that helped her treat her patients.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Virginia’s Top Court Delivers a Major Victory for Republicans
item.description: Also, what health experts want you to know about hantavirus. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Voters at a polling location in Arlington, Va., on April 21, 2026.

item.title: Proposed Rule by Postal Service Could Allow Handguns to Be Mailed
item.description: The proposal comes after the Justice Department said this year that a century-old law banning the practice was unconstitutional. Democratic leaders are calling it “unlawful.”
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Postal Service worker delivering a package to a home in the Bronx, New York.

item.title: What Our Reporter Saw in Iowa During Vance’s Splashy, 2028-Coded Trip
item.description: The vice president dropped into the state to stump for a vulnerable House Republican and, maybe, lay a little groundwork for a potential presidential run.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance leaving Des Moines this week.

item.title: Alex Zanardi, Racer Who Made Daring Comeback After Crash, Dies at 59
item.description: After losing both legs, the Italian champion persevered in auto racing. He also became a hand-cycling marathoner and Paralympian.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

media:content.media:description: Zanardi riding in his handcycle during the Rome Marathon in 2016.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Lucky Strike of Building a Bowling Monopoly
item.description: The suit, filed by bowlers from several states, accuses Lucky Strike Entertainment of driving up prices and diminishing customers’ experiences across the country.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

media:content.media:credit: Evan Sung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lucky Strike’s alley in Times Square is one of the company’s more than 360 locations in North America.

item.title: Justice Dept. Settles Case Against Provider of Meat Industry Data
item.description: Under the agreement, Agri Stats would pay a fine and broaden whom it sold its pricing data to. The department said the move would help reduce food costs.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Draper

media:content.media:description: The Justice Department said meat processors had used information exchanges run by Agri Stats to limit supply and raise prices.

item.title: Mother’s Day: Readers Share the Best Advice From Their Moms
item.description: Readers share thoughtful, practical and often funny advice from their moms.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Pearson

item.title: U.F.O. Files Released by U.S. Shed Light on What the Government Knows
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released online what it called “new, never-before-seen” files, dating back decades, related to unidentified flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: An image of a U.F.O. resembling a football-shaped body near Japan that the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command reported.

item.title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary, commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, at the White House last year.

item.title: Pentagon Releases U.F.O. Files
item.description: The Pentagon released “new, never-before-seen” U.F.O. files on Friday. The files include murky videos and still images that do not show anything definitive. The Defense Department said new materials would be released on a rolling basis.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
